# Generated Files

Generated files are required for a real board, but they are not part of the public framework release.

## HPM

For an HPM board, create a local board package:

```text
Clight/platforms/hpm/boards/<board_name>/
  board.c
  board.h
  clock.c
  clock.h
  pinmux.c
  pinmux.h
  CMakeLists.txt
  <board_name>.yaml
  <board_name>.cfg
```

Use the HPM VSCode pin assignment and board tools to generate or update those files. Then point your local HPM platform CMake to `<board_name>`.

Do not publish `.hpmpc` files. They can contain local machine data and HPM tool credentials such as client keys or secret keys.

## STM32

For STM32, generate a CubeMX project locally and keep the generated package private:

```text
Clight/platforms/stm32/board/<board_name>/
  Core/
  Drivers/
  startup_*.s
  *.ld
  CMakeLists.txt
  cmake/
```

The Clight STM32 BSP can use the HAL handles and init functions generated by CubeMX, but the public framework does not commit CubeMX `Core/Drivers` output.

If a product wants to publish a reusable board package, publish it as a separate board repository or package, not mixed with the core framework.

## ESP32

ESP32 uses ESP-IDF project generation and configuration locally.

Keep these local:

```text
Clight/platforms/esp32/sdkconfig
Clight/platforms/esp32/build/
Clight/platforms/esp32/managed_components/
```

Commit only generic source templates. If you need a board-specific `sdkconfig.defaults`, keep it in the product repository unless it is truly generic and contains no credentials.

Wi-Fi SSID, passwords, BLE keys, tokens, and certificates must never be committed.

## Vendor Stacks

Protocol stacks and vendor packages can be used locally through `vendor/`, SDK environment variables, or product-level CMake. The public framework should not upload:

- STM32Cube HAL source trees
- HPM SDK source trees
- ESP-IDF build output
- EtherCAT SSC generated vendor files
- lwIP copied source trees unless the license and release policy are explicitly handled
- binary tools or local generated firmware images
